title: Expose opaque product IDs in storefront contracts
verify: Catalog and product-detail responses add the canonical opaque Medusa product id.
Storefront CatalogProduct and ProductDetail types accept and preserve product id.
No client derives product identity from handle, variant ID, title, or SKU.
evidence_required: Additive backend contract assertions for both endpoints.
Catalog and product-detail regression smoke output.
Workspace typecheck and Memory Bank lint output.
constraints: Change is additive to existing catalog/detail contracts.
Expose only opaque canonical product ID, not internal query details.
Preserve all FT-001/FT-002 behavior and response fields.
invariants: Wishlist product identity is Medusa Product ID.
Handle remains navigation, not durable favorite identity.
Variant IDs remain variant selection/cart identity only.
purpose: Provide the opaque product identity required by wishlist mutations without replacing current catalog/detail contracts.
success_outcome: Both product surfaces expose canonical product IDs and retain all existing catalog/detail semantics.
anti_goals: Do not change handles or variant identity.

stop_conditions: Canonical product ID is unavailable through existing query results.
Additive field causes a breaking catalog/detail contract change.
Implementation requires client-derived identity from handle/SKU/title.
